title: Advanced Arduino
subtitle: Sensors, Signals & Smart Code!
overview: This session dives deeper into Arduino programming and hardware integration — covering analog vs digital signals, PWM, sensors, and serial communication to build smarter, interactive projects.

images._note: Add photos from the actual session — sensor demos, wiring diagrams, student projects, serial monitor screenshots.

gallery.caption: Arduino UNO — The Smart Brain of our circuit!

gallery.caption: Analog = Smooth wave | Digital = ON/OFF steps

gallery.caption: PWM — Rapidly blinking to fake in-between brightness!

gallery.caption: HC-SR04 — Sends sound, measures echo time = distance!

gallery.caption: LDR — Resistance drops when light shines on it

gallery.caption: Serial Monitor — Arduino talking back to your computer!

gallery.caption: Servo Motor — Angle controlled by code (0°–180°)

keyTopics: Digital vs Analog Signals — ON/OFF (0 or 1) vs Smooth Range (0–1023)
PWM (Pulse Width Modulation) — Faking analog with rapid ON/OFF pulses
analogRead() — Reading sensor values (LDR, potentiometer, temperature)
analogWrite() — Controlling LED brightness and motor speed via PWM
Ultrasonic Sensor (HC-SR04) — Sound pulse → echo → distance calculation
Serial Monitor — Arduino sending data back to computer for debugging
Functions in Arduino — void setup(), void loop(), and custom functions
Servo Motor Control — Sweep 0° to 180° using the Servo library

activity.title: Smart Distance Alarm with Ultrasonic Sensor + LED + Buzzer
parts: USB cable + laptop with Arduino IDE
steps: Wire HC-SR04: VCC → 5V, GND → GND, TRIG → Pin 9, ECHO → Pin 10
Connect LED (with 220Ω resistor) to Pin 13 and GND
Connect buzzer positive leg to Pin 8, negative to GND
Open Arduino IDE and include the distance formula: distance = duration * 0.034 / 2
Code: If distance < 20 cm → LED ON + Buzzer beeps FAST
Code: If distance 20–50 cm → LED blinks slowly
Code: If distance > 50 cm → LED OFF + Buzzer silent
Open Serial Monitor (9600 baud) to watch live distance values
Challenge: Add a second threshold and change buzzer tone by distance!

quiz.q: What is a Digital signal in Arduino?
options: A signal with smooth, continuous values
A signal that is either fully ON (1) or fully OFF (0)
A signal that only measures temperature
quiz.explanation: Digital = ON or OFF, 1 or 0! Like a light switch — it's either up or down, no in-between.

quiz.q: What range of values does analogRead() return on an Arduino UNO?
quiz.explanation: analogRead() gives values from 0 to 1023 (10-bit resolution). 0 = 0V, 1023 = 5V.
PWM = Pulse Width Modulation! It rapidly switches ON/OFF to fake an analog output — used for LED dimming and motor speed.

quiz.q: Which Arduino pins support PWM output (marked with ~)?
quiz.explanation: On Arduino UNO, PWM is available on pins 3, 5, 6, 9, 10, 11 (marked with ~ on the board).

quiz.q: What value do you pass to analogWrite() for 50% brightness (half power)?
quiz.explanation: analogWrite() takes 0–255. Half of 255 ≈ 127. So analogWrite(pin, 127) = ~50% brightness!

quiz.q: What does the HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor use to measure distance?
quiz.explanation: HC-SR04 sends a sound pulse from TRIG, then measures how long the echo takes to return on ECHO. Time → Distance!

quiz.q: What is the distance formula used with HC-SR04?
quiz.explanation: distance (cm) = duration × 0.034 / 2. We divide by 2 because sound travels TO the object AND back!

quiz.q: What is the Serial Monitor used for in Arduino?
options: To display text and sensor values sent from Arduino to the computer
quiz.explanation: Serial Monitor lets Arduino 'talk' to your computer! Use Serial.println() to print values — great for debugging!

quiz.q: What baud rate is most commonly used with Serial.begin()?
quiz.explanation: Serial.begin(9600) is the most common baud rate for Arduino projects. Both the code and Serial Monitor must match!

quiz.q: What does void loop() do in Arduino code?
options: Runs once when the Arduino powers on
Runs forever in a continuous loop as long as Arduino has power
quiz.explanation: void loop() runs over and over, forever! It's the heart of Arduino — like a beating heart that never stops.

quiz.q: Which library is used to control a Servo Motor in Arduino?
quiz.explanation: #include <Servo.h> lets you control servo motor angle easily. Use myServo.write(90) to move to 90 degrees!

quiz.q: What angle range can a standard servo motor rotate to?
quiz.explanation: Standard servo motors rotate from 0° to 180°. Use myServo.write(0) for full left, write(180) for full right!

quiz.q: An LDR (Light Dependent Resistor) behaves how?
options: Resistance increases when light is bright
It only works with digital signals
quiz.explanation: LDR = Light Dependent Resistor! More light → less resistance → more current flows. Dark → high resistance!

quiz.q: What is a 'function' in Arduino programming?
options: A named block of reusable code that performs a specific task
quiz.explanation: Functions are reusable code blocks! Instead of repeating code, write a function once and call it anywhere. Saves time!

quiz.q: What happens if you connect an LED directly to 5V without a resistor?
options: LED burns out due to too much current
quiz.explanation: Without a resistor, too much current flows through the LED and it BURNS OUT instantly! Always use a 220Ω resistor to protect it.
